skill.name: higgsfield-generate
skill.description: Generate Higgsfield AI images, videos, marketing ads, and video virality reports through the Higgsfield CLI.
skill.summary: Route Higgsfield media requests to the right model and CLI workflow.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found most hits are false positives from markdown backticks, CLI examples, model names, and troubleshooting text, with no prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration found. The skill still carries high risk because it tells agents to install a remote script with curl piped directly to sh and then run a Bash-based CLI that uploads user media.

high_findings.title: Remote installer piped directly to shell
high_findings.description: The bootstrap step instructs agents to download an install script from GitHub and execute it with sh in one command. This is a real supply-chain risk because the script is not pinned, reviewed, or checksum verified before execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present in the bootstrap instructions. It is a known risky installation pattern, although I did not find evidence that the referenced installer is malicious.

medium_findings.title: Local media uploads to a third-party service
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents that local image, video, and audio paths are auto-uploaded by the Higgsfield CLI. This is expected for media generation, but it creates privacy and data-handling risk for user files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly states that local paths are uploaded or used to create avatars and media jobs. This is legitimate product behavior, but users need clear consent before upload.

medium_findings.title: Backend URL fetch workflows can disclose external targets
medium_findings.description: The skill supports product and brand-kit creation by sending user-provided URLs to Higgsfield services for fetching. This is legitimate functionality, but it can disclose target URLs and page content to the service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL fetch flows are clearly documented and tied to expected marketing workflows. I did not find evidence of malicious exfiltration, but the data sharing risk is real.

low_findings.title: Temporary JSON files in CLI examples
low_findings.description: Several examples create temporary JSON files for avatars and product IDs before passing them to the CLI. These are local helper files, not evidence of malicious filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are limited to mktemp examples and static JSON argument preparation. There is no indication that sensitive files are read or modified.

low_findings.title: Static weak-crypto and reconnaissance hits are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections map to model names, dimensions, status strings, or markdown text, not cryptographic code. The reconnaissance detections map to normal CLI schema and status inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation and no host reconnaissance. The cited lines are documentation for model selection and Higgsfield CLI validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to shell installation pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains curl piped directly to sh for CLI installation. Marketplace publication should require safer installation instructions, pinning, or checksum verification.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is a direct match for a dangerous supply-chain pattern and is semantically used as an installation step.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown command substitutions are mostly documentation examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Many static command-substitution detections occur inside fenced bash examples that capture Higgsfield CLI output. These are not hidden code, but they reinforce that the skill depends on Bash execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are visible shell examples in documentation, not obfuscated execution. They are operationally relevant because agents may run them during normal use.

content.user_title: Generate Higgsfield Images, Videos, and Ads
content.value_statement: Creative teams need reliable model routing for Higgsfield media jobs. This skill selects models, builds CLI commands, and returns usable media links.
actual_capabilities: Selects Higgsfield image and video models for prompt, reference image, and image-to-video requests.
Builds Higgsfield CLI generation commands with media flags, model parameters, and wait behavior.
Guides Marketing Studio workflows for ads, avatars, products, hooks, settings, and brand kits.
Supports product URL imports, custom avatars, DTC ad formats, and branded creative flows.
Routes finished videos to Virality Predictor and summarizes hook, attention, retention, and creative scores.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for authentication, validation errors, rate limits, and failed jobs.
limitations: Requires the Higgsfield CLI to be installed and authenticated before generation can run.
Uses Bash commands and may upload local media files to Higgsfield services.
Does not train Soul Characters; it routes identity work to a separate skill.
Does not replace the dedicated product photoshoot workflow for listing cards or catalog images.

use_cases.title: Launch branded ad creatives
use_cases.description: Create UGC ads, product demos, unboxing videos, and branded image ads with selected products and avatars.

use_cases.title: Produce concept visuals quickly
use_cases.description: Generate image concepts, cinematic stills, reference-driven edits, and short video clips from a clear brief.

use_cases.title: Evaluate video performance signals
use_cases.description: Analyze finished videos with Virality Predictor to review hook strength, attention, retention, and distraction risk.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 16:9 image of a modern desk setup for a productivity app launch. Use clean lighting and readable headline space.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product photo into a 9:16 social video. Add a slow camera push, soft studio lighting, and subtle product movement.

prompt_templates.title: Create a UGC ad
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15 second UGC video for this skincare product URL. Use a friendly presenter, vertical format, and a clear before-and-after hook.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze ad virality
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this finished ad video for hook strength, attention, retention, distraction risk, and practical creative improvements.

output_examples.input: Generate a vertical product demo video for a running shoe.
output_examples.output: Returns a Higgsfield video URL with a short summary of the selected model, duration, aspect ratio, and ad style.

output_examples.input: Score this completed ad video for virality.
output_examples.output: Returns the overall score, strongest attention moment, retention notes, key risks, and an Open report link when available.

output_examples.input: Create a branded square image ad from a brand kit and format choice.
output_examples.output: Returns the generated image URL and a concise note about the brand kit, format, and output settings used.

best_practices: Provide the target format, aspect ratio, duration, and audience before running generation.
Use local media only when you are comfortable uploading it to Higgsfield services.
Run discovery commands for model schemas, ad formats, products, and avatars before choosing IDs.
anti_patterns: Do not pipe remote installer scripts to the shell without reviewing or pinning the source.
Do not pass product, avatar, hook, or setting IDs that the user did not choose or approve.
Do not expose raw JSON, internal artifact URLs, or implementation details in normal user-facing results.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates Higgsfield images, videos, marketing ads, and video analysis reports through the Higgsfield CLI.

faq.question: Does it need a Higgsfield account?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI must be installed, authenticated, and able to submit jobs to the Higgsfield service.

faq.question: Can it use my images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It can pass local paths or existing IDs, and the CLI uploads local media when needed.

faq.question: Can it create UGC ads?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Marketing Studio flows for UGC, product demos, unboxing, presenters, products, hooks, and settings.

faq.question: Can it analyze existing ads?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes finished videos to Virality Predictor for attention, hook, retention, and creative scoring.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as is?
faq.answer: Not without review. The skill includes a remote installer piped to the shell, which is a supply-chain risk.
